Ensure DOB is present in a data.table

Source: R/utils.R
apply_dob.Rd

Adds a properly formatted DOB column to the main table (primary_dt), using either an existing DOB column or a supplemental patients table.

Usage

apply_dob(
  primary_dt,
  patients = NULL,
  study_id_column_name = "STUDY_ID",
  date_of_birth_column_name = "DOB",
  verbose = TRUE
)

Arguments

primary_dt

A data.table (e.g., immunization_data or visit_data).

patients

Optional data.table of patient info with STUDY_ID and DOB.

study_id_column_name

Name of the study ID column in the data (default "STUDY_ID").

date_of_birth_column_name

Name of the DOB column in the input data/patients (default "DOB").

verbose

Logical, print progress messages (default TRUE).

Value

data.table = primary_dt with a standardized DOB column of type Date, merged from patients if necessary.

Details

Cases handled:

  1. If primary_dt already has DOB as Date: trust and renamed to "DOB" if needed.

  2. Else, if patients supplied: DOB merged in by STUDY_ID, coerced to Date if needed.

  3. Else: attempt to derive DOB directly from primary_dt, coercing if needed.

Examples

library(data.table)
#> Warning: package ‘data.table’ was built under R version 4.4.3
#> data.table 1.17.8 using 11 threads (see ?getDTthreads).  
#> Latest news: r-datatable.com
visits <- data.table(STUDY_ID = 1, VISIT_DATE = Sys.Date())
pats   <- data.table(STUDY_ID = 1, DOB = as.Date("2020-01-01"))
add_patient_dob(visits, pats)
#> Error in add_patient_dob(visits, pats): could not find function "add_patient_dob"
